title: Stelis renilabia
intro: Stelis renilabia is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Stelis renilabia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Stelis renilabia is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family consists of approximately 30,000 species, making it the second-largest flowering plant family in the world. The genus Stelis is divided into several subgenera, each containing distinct species. The classification of Stelis renilabia is subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of orchid taxonomy.
